title: Existential subquery check
prompt: Write `MyApp.Accounts.users_with_any_posts/0` that returns users where there exists at least one post authored by them. Use `where: exists(subquery(q))` pattern with a subquery that selects `p.id from Post where p.user_id == parent_as(:user).id`. Name the outer binding `as: :user`. Write to `lib/my_app/accounts.ex`.
tier_rationale: Introduces `parent_as` and `exists` together; marginally above trivial because `parent_as` is non-obvious, but still a single-pattern task.
